message:
fix(nats): extract fail-closed send_server_pong method with write-failure test

The NATS client had five separate inline PONG write sequences
(write_all + flush) scattered across handshake, process, and drain
paths.  If a PONG write partially failed while self.connected was true,
the connection could remain in a half-alive state where subsequent
publishes would attempt to write to a broken stream.

Introduce send_server_pong() which:
- Temporarily clears self.connected before the write/flush
- Restores it only on success
- Leaves the client in a disconnected state on any I/O error

This fail-closed behavior ensures a broken PONG write immediately
prevents follow-up operations rather than producing silent corruption.

All five PONG write sites now delegate to send_server_pong().

New test process_ping_write_failure_marks_client_disconnected verifies:
- A PONG write against a RST-closed peer returns NatsError::Io
- The client remains disconnected after failure
- Follow-up publish returns NatsError::NotConnected

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
